asymmetric-cryptography.name: Asymmetric cryptography
asymmetric-cryptography.definition: A style of secret-keeping that uses two different keys instead of one shared password: one key locks a message and a separate, matching key unlocks it.

communication-protocol.name: Communication protocol
communication-protocol.definition: An agreed set of rules two computers follow when talking, so each one knows what kind of message to expect next and what every message means.

data-packets.name: Data packets
data-packets.definition: Small labeled chunks that a message is chopped into before being sent across a network, each traveling on its own and carrying enough information to be put back in order.

delivery-acknowledgement.name: Delivery acknowledgement
delivery-acknowledgement.definition: A short reply one computer sends back to confirm it received what the other sent, so the sender knows the message arrived and does not have to guess or resend it blindly.

modular-arithmetic.name: Modular arithmetic
modular-arithmetic.definition: A way of counting where the numbers wrap back to the start once they reach a fixed limit, the way the hours on a clock jump from 12 back to 1.

prime-numbers.name: Prime numbers
prime-numbers.definition: Whole numbers bigger than 1 that cannot be made by multiplying two smaller whole numbers together, like 2, 3, 5, and 7.

quicksort.name: Quicksort
quicksort.definition: A sorting algorithm that repeatedly partitions an array around a pivot until every piece is sorted.

rsa-encryption.name: RSA encryption
rsa-encryption.definition: A way to send secret messages using two matching keys: a public key anyone can use to lock a message, and a private key that only the owner keeps and can use to unlock it.

tcp-connection-lifecycle.name: TCP connection lifecycle
tcp-connection-lifecycle.definition: The full set of steps two computers go through to talk reliably over a network: they first greet each other to agree to start, then send messages back and forth while confirming each one arrived, and finally say goodbye to close the conversation cleanly.

unreliable-delivery.name: Unreliable delivery
unreliable-delivery.definition: The fact that a message sent across a network can quietly fail to arrive, arrive damaged, or arrive out of order, with no automatic warning to the sender.
